headline: Lakehouse proof pack shows end-to-end ingestion and curation with measurable data-quality outcomes (66.67% pass rate) across Bronze→Silver→Gold.

summary.platformFit: Delta Lake pattern validated: 12 source rows landed to Bronze (12), curated in Silver with explicit accept/reject handling (8 accepted, 4 rejected), and aggregated/served to Gold (5). Three Delta tables created, indicating a minimal but complete lakehouse footprint.
summary.qualityPosture: Quality controls are active and producing auditable results: 8/12 rows accepted, 4/12 failed (66.67% pass). Rejected rows are explicitly tracked (SilverRejectedRows=4), supporting reviewer-safe traceability and remediation workflows.
summary.handoffPosture: Ready for field engineering handoff with clear counts at each layer and a reconciled quality report matching the proof pack. Key implementation artifacts implied: Delta tables for Bronze/Silver/Gold, and a deterministic rule set driving Silver acceptance/rejection.
summary.nextAction: Provide FE with (1) the exact DQ rule definitions and failure reasons for the 4 rejected rows, (2) table names/paths and schema for the 3 Delta tables, and (3) a runbook to reprocess after fixes and target a higher pass rate (e.g., ≥95%) plus row-count reconciliation checks.
